$comment: Known-good signed-CSRF token per SPEC-0001 §7.3. Cross-language implementations (auth-service Java SignedCsrfSupport, api-gateway Lua bff-session, future Quarkus/Micronaut variants) must produce byte-identical output for the inputs below. Auth service's CsrfFixtureTest reads this file and asserts SignedCsrfSupport.validate accepts the signed_token; any new implementation should add an equivalent test.

algorithm.key_encoding: standard Base64 (java.util.Base64.getDecoder applied to signing_key_base64 yields the raw key bytes)

inputs.signing_key_comment: 32 zero bytes encoded as standard Base64. Production keys MUST be cryptographically random; this fixture uses a deterministic key so any implementer can reproduce the expected output by hand.
